summary.intent: Fix a bug in the pagination component where controlled page state was being mutated during page size changes, causing desynchronization from the parent's prop, as flagged by a Codex review on PR #666.
summary.outcome: The fix was implemented and merged: pagination now preserves the raw controlled prop and exposes a clamped effective page via Context::current_page() and Api::current_page(). A regression test for shrink-then-expand was added, all CI gates passed, and the fix was pushed as a follow-up commit on the same PR.

repo: The repo uses a Codex review bot triggered via '@codex review' PR comment; approval is signaled by a 👍 reaction, active review by 👀, and feedback by unresolved review threads.
The fast CI gate is run via 'cargo xci-fast' and covers 10 gates: fmt, check, clippy, unit tests, formatting, workspace check, mutual exclusion, and snapshot check.
Ignored proprtest suites require the '--ignored' flag and can be run with PROPTEST_CASES=10000 for thorough coverage.
The project uses a Bindable abstraction in ars-core for controlled/uncontrolled state management in components.
PR workflow: push branch → open PR → trigger '@codex review' → poll for 👍 or unresolved threads → address threads → push fix commit.

code.finding: Controlled page Bindable was being clamped/mutated directly rather than preserving the raw prop. The fix separates raw controlled state from the clamped effective page exposed via context and API.
Current page access in Context and Api was reading raw state; after the fix it routes through an effective_page calculation to handle out-of-range controlled values gracefully.
Proptest state machine for pagination; a new regression case for shrink-then-expand was added here to prevent recurrence of the controlled-page desync bug.

workflow: Codex review polling requires watching both reactions (👀/👍) and unresolved thread count via GitHub GraphQL API; a script with sleep-60 polling loop is the standard approach in this repo.
Post-review code changes on a pushed PR require rerunning the focused proptest and the full fast gate before pushing the follow-up commit.
cargo +nightly fmt must be run and checked before committing; clippy with -D warnings is enforced per crate.
Commit messages follow conventional commits format: 'fix(scope): description'.

friction: Commit hook ran quietly for an extended period after a large test-heavy change, requiring passive waiting.
Codex review polling required multiple manual re-polls and direct API calls after the automated script timed out without a signal, adding latency to the review cycle.
A clippy warning was introduced in the edited path during the initial fix and required a separate correction pass before the follow-up commit.
cargo +nightly fmt had to be applied interactively (fmt --check failed, then fmt was run, then fmt --check re-verified), adding extra round-trips.
open_items: The Codex review thread resolution status after the fix commit was pushed is not confirmed in the transcript — thread may still need to be marked resolved.
PR #666 CI completion and final merge are not shown; the PR may still be pending full CI and approval.
The broader question of whether other components using Bindable have the same controlled-prop mutation pattern was not audited.
